findings: Sidebar uses <nav> with aria-label='Main navigation' and skip-to-content link
MetricCard trend indicators include directional icon AND text alongside color — not color-only
DataTable provides <caption>, scoped <th> headers, and aria-sort='ascending|descending|none' on sortable columns
Sort state changes announced via aria-live='polite' region
NotificationDrawer uses <dialog> element with focus trap, Escape-to-close, and focus-restore-to-trigger
All interactive elements have visible 3px focus ring with 2px offset
Touch targets meet 44x44px minimum on all clickable elements
Chart components include text summaries and data-table alternatives for screen reader users
Responsive reflow works down to 320px width without horizontal scrolling
Color contrast: sidebar text (#94A3B8) on #1E293B meets 4.58:1 ratio for AA large text
gaps: Loading skeleton animations should respect prefers-reduced-motion
Live region announcements for bulk actions need debouncing to avoid announcement floods

domain_boundaries: features/dashboard/ — KPI grid, charts, metric cards (read-heavy, no mutations)
features/filters/ — filter state, saved presets, date-range selection
features/notifications/ — drawer UI, unread state, optimistic dismissal
shared/ui/ — AppShell, Button, Badge, SkeletonLoader, Toast
shared/api/ — typed API client with request/response contracts
state_management: URL search params for current filters (bookmarkable, shareable)
React Context for notification drawer open/close state
React Query (TanStack) for server-state caching and refetch

adopted: Semantic color tokens used across all components — no raw hex values in feature code
Typography scale applied consistently; mixed font families for metrics vs prose
Spacing tokens used for all padding, margin, and gap values
Badge variants: neutral|success|warning|danger, with and without dot indicator
gaps: No design-token package published to npm — tokens copied manually into theme config
SkeletonLoader component not yet part of shared design system; duplicated across features
No automated drift detection between design files and implementation tokens

observations: Primary action (Export) clearly positioned in filter bar with high-contrast CTA
KPI grid allows at-a-glance scanning with consistent metric card layout
Filter state preserved in URL — users can share filtered views via link
Notification drawer provides per-item dismiss and bulk 'mark all read'
Error states show inline recovery action (retry button) rather than just error text
Skeleton loading provides spatial stability — no layout shift on data arrival
concerns: No undo for bulk actions (delete, mark-read) — irreversible without confirmation dialog
Date range picker is custom-built rather than native input[type=date]; keyboard accessibility needs testing
Export action provides no progress feedback for long-running report generation jobs

findings: Role-based UI gating: admin-only export and bulk-delete actions hidden from viewer roles
Account table redacts PII fields (email, phone) for users with viewer role — shows masked values
Audit events logged for every export action and bulk operation
Notification drawer fetches from role-scoped endpoint — users only see their own notifications
API routes validate authorization on server; client-side hiding is UX convenience only

tests: MetricCard renders positive/negative/neutral trends correctly with appropriate aria labels
DataTable sort triggers api call with correct params and announces sort change
FilterPanel applies filters, preserves state in URL, and clears correctly
NotificationDrawer traps focus, closes on Escape, and restores focus to trigger
axe-core accessibility scan passes with zero violations on all routes
Visual regression test for desktop (1440px) and tablet (1024px) breakpoints
performance: AppShell LCP under 1.8s (sidebar + top bar + first KPI row)
DataTable renders 500 rows at under 80ms with virtualization
No Cumulative Layout Shift on data load — skeleton dimensions match final content
Notification drawer content deferred (lazy-loaded on first open)
maintainability: Components named by function (MetricCard, FilterPanel) not visual style (BlueCard, TopBar)
Design tokens referenced via CSS custom properties — no raw values in component styles
Each feature module under 300 lines; shared UI components under 150 lines each
State machines documented for complex UI (drawer open/close, export flow) in feature README
